title: physostemon lanceolatus subsp. paraguensis
intro: Physostemon lanceolatus subsp. paraguensis is a plant species in the family Anacardiaceae, also known as the cashew family, native to South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Physostemon lanceolatus subsp. paraguensis is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is typically combined with other plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
